prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='DWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='NaiveSeasonal', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.72) and high seasonality variance (0.79), with early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS) differing from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost, rf). The overall champion is rf (lowest RMSE), while DWT_catboost leads in trend tracking and NaiveSeasonal excels at seasonality capture. These divergent strengths across models strongly support DBA combination as the recommended approach to leverage complementary model behaviors.

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "DWT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "NaiveSeasonal",
  "overall_champion": "rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "DWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["rf", "CWT_rf", "DWT_rf", "DWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "FT_rf", "FT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_FT_catboost", "FT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "CWT_catboost", "DWT_svr", "THETA", "ONLY_FT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.7228,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.72) and high seasonality variance (0.79), with early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS) differing from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost, rf). The overall champion is rf (lowest RMSE), while DWT_catboost leads in trend tracking and NaiveSeasonal excels at seasonality capture. These divergent strengths across models strongly support DBA combination as the recommended approach to leverage complementary model behaviors."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35": {"shrinkage": 0.35}, "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5": {"top_k": 5}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong model diversity across horizons. relative_spread_mean=0.134 shows moderate disagreement. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality_variance=0.79 and trend_champions like DWT_catboost/rf differ from seasonality_champions like NaiveSeasonal/THETA. With n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting. RMSE spread_ratio=0.7228 justifies rmse_focus preset. Top-k selection captures horizon heterogeneity while inverse_rmse_weights provides robust weighting."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set ['best_per_horizon_by_validation', 'dba_combination', 'inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35', 'topk_mean_per_horizon_k5'] has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35) = 3 unique types. No leakage detected - all candidates use safe strategies (baselines never leak; weighted/selection use anti-leakage expanding windows per eval_config). Current best inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 has score 0.8007 with RMSE 31481, MAPE 0.0811, SMAPE 0.0823, POCID 69.70. Universe leaderboards show candidates like topk_mean_per_horizon_k8 (RMSE 30956) but they have lower overall scores in candidate_ranking_top and aren't needed for diversity. Set size of 4 is appropriate. No changes required.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.72) and high seasonality variance (0.79), with early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS) differing from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost, rf). The overall champion is rf (lowest RMSE), while DWT_catboost leads in trend tracking and NaiveSeasonal excels at seasonality capture. These divergent strengths across models strongly support DBA combination as the recommended approach to leverage complementary model behaviors.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong model diversity across horizons. relative_spread_mean=0.134 shows moderate disagreement. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality_variance=0.79 and trend_champions like DWT_catboost/rf differ from seasonality_champions like NaiveSeasonal/THETA. With n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting. RMSE spread_ratio=0.7228 justifies rmse_focus preset. Top-k selection captures horizon heterogeneity while inverse_rmse_weights provides robust weighting.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set ['best_per_horizon_by_validation', 'dba_combination', 'inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35', 'topk_mean_per_horizon_k5'] has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35) = 3 unique types. No leakage detected - all candidates use safe strategies (baselines never leak; weighted/selection use anti-leakage expanding windows per eval_config). Current best inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 has score 0.8007 with RMSE 31481, MAPE 0.0811, SMAPE 0.0823, POCID 69.70. Universe leaderboards show candidates like topk_mean_per_horizon_k8 (RMSE 30956) but they have lower overall scores in candidate_ranking_top and aren't needed for diversity. Set size of 4 is appropriate. No changes required.
